title: Published CLI contract points at a missing `openclaude` entrypoint
reasoning: The package manifest still advertises an `openclaude` bin at `./bin/openclaude`, but the rest of the repo has already been switched to `duckhive`: the build only produces `dist/cli.mjs`, the native installer launches `bin/duckhive`, and the local-installer helper writes and probes `duckhive` rather than `openclaude`. There is no build step here that creates `bin/openclaude`, so the published package and the installer surfaces do not agree on the executable name. That is a release blocker for the CLI contract: users installing the npm package will not reliably get the command the package metadata promises.
recommendation: Pick one public command name and make every surface match it. If `openclaude` is still the supported CLI, ship a real bin wrapper or point the bin field at the built CLI entrypoint and update the local installer helpers accordingly; if the rename to `duckhive` is intentional, remove the stale `openclaude` bin contract and update the affected tests/docs together.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The nearby tests only check helper-returned path strings and local-detection heuristics; they do not verify that the packed npm artifact contains a runnable executable at the advertised bin target or that the build emits that target.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a packaging-level test that inspects the built/package-packed manifest and asserts the advertised bin path exists in the tarball, plus a unit test that `getLocalClaudePath()` and `getCandidateLocalBinaryPaths()` resolve the same command name as the package bin.
minimumFixScope: package.json, scripts/build.ts, scripts/install.sh, src/utils/localInstaller.ts, and the path/install surface tests that encode the chosen command name.
